title: oldenlandia affinis subsp. affinis
intro: Oldenlandia affinis subsp. affinis, also known as the small-flowered oldenlandia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is traditionally used in medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and fight off infections, leading to its potential health benefits in promoting overall well-being. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Oldenlandia affinis subsp. affinis are often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions to harness its medicinal properties.
